evidence_summary: Establish the overwhelming scale of an ancient, extinct civilization through white marble and gold architecture. Contrast this grandeur with the brutal, callous biological reality of the current Martian inhabitants to shift the tone from awe to dread.

The narrator and Tars Tarkas arrive at a grand, ancient city built by an extinct race. As they move through the streets, the narrator observes the biological differences of the Martians and their callous attitude toward death and violence.
Wide view of grand, ancient white marble structures near a dried sea.
Close proximity to Martians exhibiting callous behavior toward violence.
Walking through the streets and observing the biological/cultural differences of Martians.
